title: Memorandum of Understanding between the Royal Canadian Mounted Police and the Public Health Agency of Canada regarding Law Enforcement Record Checks in support of the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act Security Clearance Program - Canada.ca
content:
## Memorandum of Understanding between the Royal Canadian Mounted Police and the Public Health Agency of Canada regarding Law Enforcement Record Checks in support of the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act Security Clearance Program

## With which third party is the Public Health Agency of Canada sharing personal information
Under this Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC)'s Centre for Biosecurity through the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act (HPTA) Security Clearance Program, shares personal information to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP). This two-way determinate disclosure agreement came into effect March 21, 2024, and will end on March 31, 2028, unless PHAC and the RCMP agree to end it at an earlier date.

## Why is the Public Health Agency of Canada sharing personal information
The HPTA and the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations (HPTR) permits PHAC to conduct verifications, checks and assessments of Applicants seeking an HPTA Security Clearance. PHAC uses personal information to support decisions to grant, refuse, suspend, revoke or appeal an HPTA Security Clearance. PHAC collects personal information under sections 33 to 35 of the HPTA and sections 11 to 22 of the HPTR.
The HPTA Security Clearance process requires a Law Enforcement Record Check (LERC) under subsection 13(1)(b) of the HPTR. This MOU allows PHAC to share information with the RCMP to complete a LERC. A LERC checks whether a person has a criminal record, is associated with any criminal groups, or has any vulnerabilities related to high-risk behaviours. Each applicant consents to allow PHAC to share their information with the RCMP for this process. In certain cases, the RCMP may also share this information with other law enforcement agencies to complete a LERC. Subsection 39(1)(a) of HPTA, subsection 5(1) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act, and subsections 57(1)(c), 57(3) and 57(4) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police allow this information sharing.

## What personal information is the Public Health Agency of Canada sharing
The personal information shared by the PHAC may include:
- Full legal name (at birth, currently and previous)
- Current home address
- Home addresses for previous five years
- Phone number(s) for previous five years
- Spouse date of birth
- Consent form for the Law enforcement agencies
- Applicant's photo from the applicant's ID provided during the application (passport, driver's licence, health card, etc.)
Info Source describes how PHAC manages your personal information  at infosource.gc.ca . Refer to the Personal Information Bank Human Pathogens and Toxins Act Security Clearance Program - PHAC PPU 308 .

## Who can I contact for more information
The HPTA Screening team hpta.screening-filtrage.lapht@phac-aspc.gc.ca
